NLTS Hamiltonians from classical LTCs
papers.tldr: This work provides a completely self-contained construction of a family of NLTS Hamiltonians based on ideas from Anshu, Breuckmann, and Nirkhe and removes the constant-rate requirement from the construction of Anshu, Breuckmann, and Nirkhe.

papers.title: Circuit lower bounds for quantum code Hamiltonians and an almost linear NLTS theorem
papers.tldr: New techniques are provided, based on entropic and local indistinguishability arguments, for proving circuit lower bounds for the low-energy states of local Hamiltonians arising from quantum error-correcting codes, and it is shown that complex entanglement at high energies can persist in physically relevant systems.

papers.title: NLTS Hamiltonians and Strongly-Explicit SoS Lower Bounds from Low-Rate Quantum LDPC Codes
papers.tldr: A new method of planting a strongly explicit nontrivial codeword in linear-distance qLDPC codes, which in turn yields strongly explicit SoS lower bounds is introduced, which provides a new way of ensuring aqLDPC code has positive dimension without resorting to parity check counting, and therefore provide more flexibility in the code construction.

papers.title: Quantum Codes, Local Testability and Interactive Proofs: State of the Art and Open Questions (Invited Talk)
papers.tldr: The study of multiprover interactive proof systems, of locally testable codes, and of property testing are deeply linked, conceptually if not formally, through their role in the proof of the PCP theorem in complexity theory, and the connections between them are explained.

A gap in the slice rank of k-tensors
papers.tldr: It is proved that the asymptotic slice rank of any $k$-tensor in any field is either $1$ or at least $k/(k-1)^{( k-1)/k}$, providing evidence that straight-forward application of the slice-rank method cannot give useful results in certain problems for which non-trivial exponential bounds are already known.
